title: Fix illegal assignment of constant to unpacked array
body:
Hi,

we found a bug in heavily parametrized RTL. Parameters are mixtures of packed and unpacked arrays.
This is very reduced example, so don't try to follow the code semantics. Much of the "surroundings"
needed to "make sense" were removed.

```
module t_non_const_bug #(
    parameter logic [31 : 0] G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK[2] = {32'h0007_FFFF, 32'h0000_FFFF}
);

 logic [1:0]  dmi_addr_match;

 generate
    genvar i;
    for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin
      assign dmi_addr_match[i] = &(G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK[i]);
    end
  endgenerate

endmodule;

module t_top;

    localparam logic[31:0]      C_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK_FSS        = 32'h0007_FFFF;
    localparam logic[31:0]      C_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK_OTPSS      = 32'h0000_FFFF;

    t_non_const_bug #(
        // If I comment the following line and rely on default value of the parameters
        // the Verilation works fine.
        .G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK   ({C_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK_FSS, C_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK_OTPSS})
    ) i_t_non_const_bug ();

endmodule;
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t_non_const_bug.sv:2:30: Operator VAR 'G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK' expects 32 bits on the Initial value, but Initial value's CONST '64'h7ffff0000ffff' generates 64 bits.
                                            : ... note: In instance 't_top.i_t_non_const_bug'
    2 |     parameter logic [31 : 0] G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK[2] = {32'h0007_FFFF, 32'h0000_FFFF}
      |                              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                     ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHTRUNC?v=5.043
                     ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHTRUNC */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: t_non_const_bug.sv:10:56: Illegal assignment of constant to unpacked array
                                : ... note: In instance 't_top.i_t_non_const_bug'
   10 |       assign dmi_addr_match[i] = &(G_DMI_ADDRESSES_MASK[i]);
      |                                                        ^
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.043 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s), 1 warning(s)
```

I think these are two separate issues. The `WIDTHTRUNC` should not occur IMHO. Please
let me know if I should open another issue for this one.

The main issue is the Error. I don't think the assignment of the constant to the unpacked array
should be illegal.

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator --cc t_non_const_bug.sv
```
What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

```
v5.042-50-gc5f8656aa
```
This should be master from yesterday.

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux my-PC 6.8.0-87-generic #88~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Tue Oct 14 14:03:14 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Yes, but again, I can't guarantee I will have time, or will be able to fix the issue.
